title: Efficient Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models with Low-Rank Adapters
abstract: We study parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods that inject small trainable matrices into frozen transformer weights, reducing memory usage while retaining downstream task performance across several benchmarks.

title: A Survey of Retrieval-Augmented Generation Techniques
abstract: This survey categorizes approaches that combine dense retrieval with generative language models, comparing indexing strategies, retriever training objectives, and their effect on factual accuracy.

title: Contrastive Pretraining for Sentence Embeddings
abstract: We propose a contrastive objective for learning sentence-level representations that improves semantic similarity search without requiring labeled paraphrase pairs.

title: Vision Transformers for Small Dataset Regimes
abstract: We examine how patch-based transformer architectures perform when trained from scratch on limited image datasets, and propose data augmentation strategies to close the gap with convolutional networks.

title: Self-Supervised Depth Estimation from Monocular Video
abstract: A framework that learns to predict per-pixel depth from unlabeled video sequences by enforcing photometric consistency between adjacent frames.

title: Diffusion Models for Image Inpainting
abstract: We adapt denoising diffusion probabilistic models to the inpainting task, conditioning the reverse process on unmasked pixels to produce coherent completions.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Fraud Detection in Financial Transactions
abstract: We model transaction networks as graphs and apply message-passing neural networks to identify anomalous subgraphs indicative of fraudulent activity.

title: Anomaly Detection in Network Traffic Using Autoencoders
abstract: An unsupervised approach that learns a compressed representation of normal network flow data, flagging traffic with high reconstruction error as potentially malicious.

title: Detecting Prompt Injection Attacks in LLM Applications
abstract: We introduce a two-stage detection pipeline combining pattern-based heuristics with a secondary language model classifier to identify adversarial instructions embedded in user input.

title: Adversarial Robustness of Deep Neural Networks: A Review
abstract: We review methods for generating adversarial examples and defending against them, discussing the trade-off between robustness and clean-data accuracy.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Warehouse Robot Path Planning
abstract: We train a policy using proximal policy optimization to navigate a simulated warehouse environment, minimizing travel time while avoiding collisions with dynamic obstacles.

title: Offline Reinforcement Learning from Logged Bandit Feedback
abstract: We study policy learning from historical interaction logs without further environment access, addressing distribution shift through conservative value estimation.

title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Mobile Keyboard Prediction
abstract: A system that trains a shared language model across many devices without centralizing raw user text, aggregating model updates using secure averaging.

title: Knowledge Distillation for Deploying Compact Language Models
abstract: We transfer the predictive behavior of a large teacher model into a smaller student model, enabling low-latency inference on resource-constrained devices.

title: Multimodal Fusion for Video Captioning
abstract: We combine visual, audio, and text features using cross-attention layers to generate natural language descriptions of short video clips.

title: Explainable AI Techniques for Credit Scoring Models
abstract: We compare feature attribution methods for tree-based and neural credit risk models, evaluating their usefulness for regulatory compliance and customer communication.

title: Time Series Forecasting with Temporal Convolutional Networks
abstract: We propose a dilated causal convolution architecture for multi-step forecasting that captures long-range dependencies more efficiently than recurrent baselines.

title: Semantic Segmentation for Autonomous Driving in Adverse Weather
abstract: We evaluate segmentation model robustness under fog, rain, and low-light conditions, and propose a domain adaptation technique using synthetic weather augmentation.

title: Instruction Tuning for Aligning Language Models with User Intent
abstract: We fine-tune a pretrained language model on a curated set of instruction-response pairs, improving its ability to follow open-ended natural language commands.

title: Vector Database Indexing Strategies for Large-Scale Similarity Search
abstract: We benchmark approximate nearest neighbor indexing structures, including graph-based and quantization-based methods, for retrieval latency and recall trade-offs.

title: Speech Emotion Recognition Using Spectrogram-Based CNNs
abstract: We convert raw audio into mel-spectrograms and train a convolutional classifier to detect emotional states from short speech segments.

title: Zero-Shot Text Classification with Natural Language Inference Models
abstract: We reformulate topic classification as an entailment task, allowing a pretrained NLI model to classify text into arbitrary label sets without task-specific training.

title: Neural Architecture Search for Edge Device Deployment
abstract: We search over a constrained space of lightweight architectures under latency and memory budgets, using a differentiable search strategy to accelerate the process.

title: Detecting Machine-Generated Text with Statistical Fingerprints
abstract: We analyze token-level probability distributions to distinguish text generated by language models from human-written text, without access to the generating model.

title: Causal Inference for A/B Testing in Recommendation Systems
abstract: We apply causal graph methods to correct for selection bias in observational recommendation data, improving the reliability of offline experiment evaluation.

title: Continual Learning Without Catastrophic Forgetting
abstract: We propose a regularization method that constrains updates to parameters important for previously learned tasks, enabling sequential task learning in a single model.

title: Prompt Engineering Strategies for Few-Shot Reasoning Tasks
abstract: We systematically compare chain-of-thought, self-consistency, and retrieval-augmented prompting strategies on multi-step arithmetic and logical reasoning benchmarks.

title: Synthetic Data Generation for Low-Resource Named Entity Recognition
abstract: We use a generative language model to create labeled training examples for entity recognition in domains with scarce annotated data.

title: Energy-Efficient Training of Deep Learning Models
abstract: We measure the carbon footprint of common training pipelines and evaluate techniques such as mixed-precision training and gradient checkpointing for reducing energy consumption.

title: Cross-Lingual Transfer for Low-Resource Machine Translation
abstract: We leverage shared multilingual representations to improve translation quality for language pairs with limited parallel corpora.
